approval_notes: Owner instructed 'take next task' and, when asked to confirm scope, delegated the decision ('work autonomously and make good decisions'). The Chief scoped this as the final F003 slice and is implementing it under the standing autonomy boundaries (may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open PR; may NOT merge, flip passes:true, or add dependencies).

This is the THIRD and final F003 slice. Slice 1 (configuration system, CODEX task 003 = acceptance step 1) merged as PR #9 (squash 3b7e0b1). Slice 2 (domain model + Alembic migrations, task 004 = acceptance steps 2 and 3) merged as PR #10 (squash 7ec4257). Slice 3 = the Z0 classification engine (task 005 = acceptance step 4).
F003 stays passes:false after this slice. Flipping F003 passes:true requires a fresh-context FULL-EPIC Level 2 evaluation (config + domain model + Z0 engine together), exactly as F002 was closed out.
No new runtime dependency: the engine is pure standard-library Python over the vocabularies already defined in apps/api/app/models/vocab.py.
objective: Introduce the Z0 classification engine: a pure, deterministic function that maps an offer's material facts (offer type, card requirement, paid dependencies, eligibility, availability window, and per-quota exhaustion behaviour) to an explainable zero-cost class (Z0_TRUE_FREE, Z1_BILLING_EXPOSURE, Z2_TEMPORARY_OR_CONDITIONAL, Z3_SELF_HOSTED_BUILDING_BLOCK, or UNKNOWN). Classification must never infer Z0 from missing or contradictory material data, must gate Z0 on the explicit absence of card/purchase/paid-dependency/automatic-billing exposure AND a safe quota-exhaustion behaviour, and must emit a human-readable explanation of the deciding reasons and blocking conditions.
scope: New package apps/api/app/classify/: engine.py (a ClassificationResult dataclass plus the deterministic classify() function and its ordered decision gates), __init__.py exports.

The engine consumes a small, explicit input structure (offer_type, requires_card: bool|None, has_paid_dependencies: bool|None, eligibility, available_from/until, and the set/list of quota exhaustion_behaviours). Nullable booleans (None) and 'unknown' exhaustion behaviour represent UNKNOWN material conditions.

Decision rules mirroring docs/PRODUCT_REQUIREMENTS.md and docs/DATA_MODEL.md: (Z1) card required, purchase required, paid dependency, or an automatic_billing exhaustion behaviour => Z1_BILLING_EXPOSURE (never Z0); (Z3) self-hosted offer types (self_hosted_open_source) => Z3_SELF_HOSTED_BUILDING_BLOCK; (Z2) trial / new_customer_credit / time-limited (available_until in the past or a bounded window) / eligibility-conditional (student/startup/hackathon/open-source programs) => Z2_TEMPORARY_OR_CONDITIONAL; (UNKNOWN) any material gate whose value is unknown (requires_card None, has_paid_dependencies None, or any quota exhaustion_behaviour == 'unknown', or no quota data at all) => UNKNOWN; (Z0) only when every billing gate is explicitly clear AND every quota exhaustion behaviour is a SAFE stop-type (hard_stop, request_rejected, throttled, service_sleeps, read_only, deployment_blocked, site_disabled_until_reset, resource_reclaimed, data_deleted) => Z0_TRUE_FREE.

Explanation output: ClassificationResult carries the assigned class, an ordered list of human-readable reasons, and a list of blocking_conditions (empty for Z0). No numeric confidence is surfaced in this slice.
A thin, read-only ORM adapter classify_offer(offer) that reads an Offer instance and its related Quota rows and returns a ClassificationResult, performing NO database writes.

tests/unit/test_z0_classifier.py (offline): a comprehensive truth table exercising each gate, each exhaustion behaviour (safe vs unsafe vs unknown), boundary and contradictory inputs, the no-quota case, and the 'no unknown material condition may yield Z0' rule; plus explanation-content assertions.
docs: a 'Z0 classification' section (in docs/DATA_MODEL.md or a short docs/Z0_CLASSIFICATION.md) documenting the gates, the safety rule, and the explanation output.
out_of_scope: Provider adapters, source scanning, document fetching, or evidence extraction (task 006+).
Persisting classifications back to the database, ScanRun orchestration, or recomputing offer.zero_cost_class at runtime.
Composing Z3 self-hosted building blocks onto verified Z0 hosting infrastructure (task 015).
Confidence-score calibration and the public confidence labels.
API routes, worker/scheduler wiring, or UI surfacing beyond the existing /health seam.
Marking F003 (or any feature) passes:true; adding managed-cloud services or heavyweight dependencies; advancing past this slice.
acceptance_criteria: classify() is a pure deterministic function (no I/O, no network, no LLM) importable offline; identical inputs always yield an identical ClassificationResult.

Billing gates: an offer with requires_card True, has_paid_dependencies True, or any quota whose exhaustion_behaviour is automatic_billing is classified Z1_BILLING_EXPOSURE and NEVER Z0, with the specific gate named in blocking_conditions.

Safety rule: any material condition that is unknown (requires_card None, has_paid_dependencies None, an exhaustion_behaviour of 'unknown', or the total absence of quota data) yields UNKNOWN and NEVER Z0.

Z0 rule: Z0_TRUE_FREE is returned only when all billing gates are explicitly clear (requires_card False, has_paid_dependencies False, no automatic_billing) AND every quota exhaustion behaviour is a safe stop-type; the result's blocking_conditions is empty and reasons explain why it is $0.

Z2 rule: trial, new_customer_credit, bounded/expired availability windows, and eligibility-conditional program offer types are classified Z2_TEMPORARY_OR_CONDITIONAL (assuming no dominating billing exposure).
Z3 rule: self_hosted_open_source offers are classified Z3_SELF_HOSTED_BUILDING_BLOCK.
Every ClassificationResult includes an ordered, human-readable reasons list; every non-Z0 result names at least one blocking or downgrading condition.
classify_offer(orm_offer) reads an Offer plus its Quota rows and returns the same result the pure function would for those facts, performing no writes.

A comprehensive truth-table unit suite (tests/unit/test_z0_classifier.py) covers each gate, each exhaustion behaviour, missing/contradictory/boundary data, and the no-Z0-on-unknown rule, and all tests pass offline.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 (pytest offline + config validation + web tests/build) and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit), including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency); the full live stack cycle stack-up -> stack-smoke -> stack-down still passes.

No unsupported free-tier/zero-cost/Z0 product claim is introduced: the engine only classifies supplied facts and defaults to UNKNOWN rather than guessing; no offer data asserting a real service is free is added.

A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict in agent-state/evaluation.json (probing missing, contradictory, and boundary data to confirm no unknown material condition yields Z0) before any status change is claimed; F003 remains passes:false (epic close-out is a separate full-epic evaluation).

end_to_end_steps: Run tests/unit/test_z0_classifier.py and confirm the full truth table passes offline.
Run scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm both pass end-to-end (no new dependency; requirements-sync green).
Bring up the live stack and confirm stack-smoke still passes (the engine adds no runtime coupling), then bring it down.
Confirm detect-secrets is green and no credential appears in code or output.
negative_tests: a quota with exhaustion_behaviour 'unknown' => UNKNOWN, not Z0.
no quota data at all => UNKNOWN, not Z0.
any quota with exhaustion_behaviour 'automatic_billing' => Z1, not Z0.
requires_card True (even with otherwise-safe quotas) => Z1, not Z0.
contradictory facts (e.g. always_free offer_type but requires_card True) => the billing gate dominates (Z1).

risks.data_migration: None. This slice adds no schema and no migration; it is a pure computation module over the slice-2 vocabularies.
risks.security_privacy: No credentials, no I/O. The engine reads only supplied facts. detect-secrets remains green.
risks.portability: Pure standard-library Python; no managed-cloud dependency; runs identically on amd64 and arm64.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The engine's central invariant is that no unknown or contradictory material condition may yield Z0; it defaults to UNKNOWN. This is the product's core anti-false-claim safeguard and is covered by adversarial truth-table tests.
risks.regression: No dependency change, so the requirements-sync guard is unaffected. The engine is additive and imported by nothing at runtime yet, so existing services and tests are unchanged.
risks.supply_chain: No new dependency (Python or npm) is added by this slice.

notes: Owner delegated the go/no-go decision to the Chief ('work autonomously and make good decisions') after being presented with the scoped slice-3 plan, acceptance criteria, deferrals, and cost estimate. This slice does not mark F003 passing; it is the final of three F003 slices and requires a fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluation that adversarially probes missing/contradictory/boundary data before its completion is claimed.
